name: Taj Mahal
overview: The Taj Mahal is one of the most famous monuments in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It was built by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal.
history: Construction of the Taj Mahal began in 1632 and took more than 20 years to complete. Around 20,000 craftsmen and artisans were employed in the project. The monument represents the peak of Mughal architectural achievement.
architecture: The Taj Mahal is built from white marble and decorated with intricate carvings, calligraphy, and precious stones. The main dome rises about 73 meters high and the complex includes gardens, mosques, and reflecting pools.
facts: The Taj Mahal changes color depending on sunlight.
It is considered one of the Seven Wonders of the World.
Over 7 million tourists visit the Taj Mahal every year.

name: Big Ben
overview: Big Ben is the nickname for the Great Bell of the clock located at the north end of the Palace of Westminster in London.
history: The clock tower was completed in 1859. It became a symbol of British democracy and parliamentary tradition. The tower was renamed Elizabeth Tower in 2012 to honor Queen Elizabeth II.
architecture: The tower stands approximately 96 meters tall and features Gothic Revival architecture designed by Augustus Pugin.
facts: The clock is one of the most accurate large clocks in the world.
Big Ben weighs more than 13 tons.
It survived bombing during World War II.

name: Colosseum
overview: The Colosseum is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built and remains one of the greatest works of Roman engineering.
history: Construction began under Emperor Vespasian and was completed by his son Titus. It hosted gladiator battles, animal hunts, and public spectacles.
architecture: The Colosseum could hold up to 50,000 spectators and features a complex system of underground tunnels known as the hypogeum.
facts: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.
Over 6 million people visit every year.

name: Eiffel Tower
overview: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower and the most famous landmark in Paris.
history: It was built for the 1889 World's Fair and initially criticized by artists and intellectuals before becoming a global symbol of France.
architecture: The tower stands 330 meters tall and was the tallest man-made structure in the world for 41 years.
facts: It was originally meant to be temporary.
Over 7 million visitors climb it annually.

name: Great Wall
overview: The Great Wall of China is a massive series of fortifications built across northern China to defend against invasions.
history: Construction started more than 2,000 years ago and continued through multiple dynasties.
architecture: The wall stretches over 21,000 kilometers and includes watchtowers, barracks, and fortresses.
facts: It is the longest wall ever built.
It is visible from space in low orbit.
